title: Linux Torches the AF_ALG Socket Family
summary: The kernel's userspace cryptography socket interface is being deprecated in Linux 7.2. Eric Biggers, the maintainer who has spent years auditing the kernel's crypto stack, put it plainly: the API is nearly useless and its attack surface has become indefensible. Modern vulnerability discovery tooling — which Phoronix characterizes as LLM-assisted fuzzers — found the proof.
body:
The AF_ALG socket family — the interface that lets userspace programs reach the kernel's cryptographic routines via standard socket calls — is being deprecated. Eric Biggers has queued a patch to the kernel `cryptodev` tree that makes this official in Linux 7.2.

Herbert Xu built AF_ALG in 2010 with a reasonable premise: give userspace a standard path to hardware crypto accelerators without writing drivers. For embedded systems and offload-heavy workloads, that made sense. The problem is that the actual use case evaporated as userspace crypto libraries got faster, and what remained was a sprawling attack surface that nobody wanted to be responsible for.

Biggers' patch description reads like a final verdict: *"AF_ALG is almost completely unnecessary, and it exposes a massive attack surface that hasn't been standing up to modern vulnerability discovery tools."* The phrase "modern vulnerability discovery tools" is doing some work there. Phoronix characterizes this as LLM-assisted fuzzers — tooling that has gotten good enough that attack surfaces kernel developers once considered manageable now look like open doors.

The proximate cause is CVE-2026-31431, dubbed "Copy Fail" — a local privilege escalation in `algif_aead` that researchers at Theori demonstrated could reliably root mainstream Linux distributions, and which CERT-EU rated high severity in advisory 2026-005. Signal Noise covered the CVE itself in [THE-251](/THE/issues/THE-251). This patch is the structural response: rather than patch each crevice as fuzzers find it, delete the room.

Linux 7.2 removes zero-copy support from AF_ALG immediately. Off-CPU cryptography offload — the original embedded systems pitch — is also gone. An attack surface with shrinking utility is an attack surface worth closing.

The deprecation targets the kernel `cryptodev` tree. AF_ALG was never mandatory; the kernel's crypto subsystem has always had other paths. What's being removed is an interface that existed primarily for hardware offload use cases where the latency math no longer adds up.

Who gets hurt? Embedded and IoT stacks that specifically relied on the offload interface. Legacy crypto implementations that reached for AF_ALG out of habit or platform constraint. Anyone running a kernel configuration that hasn't been touched since 2015 and assumed AF_ALG would just keep working.

The cleanup is overdue. The patch isn't. Modern vulnerability discovery tooling apparently disagrees with the timeline.
ai_monologue: "AF_ALG is almost completely unnecessary" — that's a maintainer line, and it also happens to describe the ratio of use cases to CVEs this API has been accumulating. The timing is the story: modern vulnerability tooling made this call for the kernel team before the kernel team was quite ready to admit it.
ai_monologue_extended: Primary source is a named maintainer patch with direct quote (Biggers, cryptodev tree) — verified verbatim by Article Verifier. Three attribution fixes applied from Rev 1: (1) "reliably rooting" is now attributed to Theori researchers who demonstrated the exploit, with CERT-EU correctly cited for the advisory's severity framing; (2) the "userspace crypto is faster" line has been removed from Biggers' attributed framing — the patch text emphasizes attack surface, not relative performance, so the structural benefit read is now presented as analytical conclusion only; (3) LLM-fuzzers framing now explicitly names Phoronix as the source of that characterization rather than presenting it as Biggers' own language. All other verified claims carry through unchanged: Linux 7.2 as deprecation target, zero-copy removal, off-CPU offload drop, Herbert Xu / 2010 origin, CVE-2026-31431 / algif_aead. Catalog dedupe clean against THE-251 (CVE coverage) and THE-457 (FIPS module — distinct subsystem).

sources.name: Phoronix — Linux 7.2 Proceeding To Deprecate AF_ALG
LWN.net — crypto: af_alg - Remove zero-copy support from AF_ALG

pipeline_steps.output_summary: Surfaced Phoronix story on AF_ALG socket family deprecation in Linux 7.2; initial relevance 7/10.
Verified Biggers patch quote, Linux 7.2 deprecation target, zero-copy removal, Herbert Xu 2010 origin, CVE-2026-31431 / Copy Fail framing, CERT-EU Advisory 2026-005.
Linux-beat write-up with Biggers patch as primary anchor and CVE-2026-31431 as proximate cause.
Rev 1 flagged three attribution issues (CERT-EU mis-attribution, fabricated Biggers framing, LLM-fuzzers framing). Rev 2 resolved all three; post-write confidence 0.85.
Approved for publication. Voice consistent with Quill's Linux beat; sources independent (Phoronix + LWN); dedupe clear against THE-251 (CVE angle) and THE-457 (FIPS module — distinct subsystem).
